seoTitle: How to Open a Microsoft Excel File in Google Sheets
metaDescription: Move your Excel workbooks into Google Sheets without breaking formulas, using Google Drive uploads and Office Compatibility Mode for cloud editing.
intro: Moving from a local Excel file to a cloud-based Google Sheet lets your team collaborate in real time, but it's easy to worry about losing formulas or table layouts in the process. Google Drive and Sheets include built-in tools for importing .xlsx files cleanly. Here's how to bring your Excel workbook into Sheets without losing your data.

steps.heading: Open Google Drive
steps.body: Sign in to your Google account and navigate to Google Drive, which serves as the storage and upload hub for any file you want to bring into Sheets.

steps.heading: Upload your Excel file
steps.body: Drag and drop your local .xlsx file directly into your Drive folder, or use the upload button, to move it into the cloud.

steps.heading: Open the file with Google Sheets
steps.body: Right-click the uploaded file and choose to open it with Google Sheets, or use Office Compatibility Mode to edit the native Excel file without converting the extension.

steps.heading: Save a Google Sheets copy if needed
steps.body: If you want a fully native Google Sheets file rather than an Excel file edited in compatibility mode, use File > Save as Google Sheets to create a converted copy.

faq.question: Will my Excel formulas still work in Google Sheets?
faq.answer: Most common formulas carry over correctly, though highly specialized Excel-only functions may need to be adjusted after import.

faq.question: Do I need to convert the file, or can I keep it as .xlsx?
faq.answer: You can keep editing it as a native .xlsx file using Office Compatibility Mode without ever changing the file extension.

faq.question: Is there a file size limit for uploading to Drive?
faq.answer: Google Drive supports large spreadsheet files, but very large workbooks with heavy formatting may take longer to process during conversion.

faq.question: Can I edit the file with others in real time after importing?
faq.answer: Yes, once the file is opened in Google Sheets, you can share it and collaborate with others in real time just like a native Sheets file.
